meta.status: POPULATED — live web research completed. DO NOT OVERWRITE — populated by research team.
sources: Belfast City Council — Roselawn Crematorium information

marketOverview.annualDeathsBelfastEstimate: ~5,000–5,800 per year (Belfast HSCT area, ~33% of NI total)
marketOverview.receptionVenueUptake: Estimated 20–30% of Belfast families now book an external venue for the wake reception (post-COVID uptake increase — many families found external venues more practical and have not reverted)
marketOverview.impliedAnnualReceptions: ~1,000–1,740 external wake receptions per year in greater Belfast
marketOverview.culturalNote: Belfast wakes have traditionally been home-based in both Catholic and Protestant traditions. Post-COVID shift toward external venues is significant and continuing. Urban south and west Belfast terraced housing makes home wakes for 60–100 guests impractical.
marketOverview.crossCommunityNote: Catholic tradition often includes meal service; Protestant tradition more commonly tea and sandwiches. Fernhill's neutral, community-owned setting serves both with equal dignity.

crematoriaAndCemeteries.name: Milltown Cemetery
crematoriaAndCemeteries.notes: Major Catholic cemetery — highest-volume west Belfast burial site. Very close to Fernhill.

crematoriaAndCemeteries.name: Belfast City Cemetery
crematoriaAndCemeteries.notes: Major BCC-operated cemetery serving west Belfast both communities.

crematoriaAndCemeteries.name: Roselawn Crematorium
crematoriaAndCemeteries.notes: Primary cremation facility for greater Belfast. Very high volume.

crematoriaAndCemeteries.name: Dundonald Cemetery
crematoriaAndCemeteries.operator: Ards and North Down Borough Council
crematoriaAndCemeteries.notes: Serves east Belfast — less direct Fernhill catchment.

currentVenueOptions.name: The Stormont Hotel
currentVenueOptions.location: Upper Newtownards Road, east Belfast BT4
currentVenueOptions.notes: Very common for east Belfast and Stormont-area families post-Roselawn.

currentVenueOptions.name: La Mon Hotel
currentVenueOptions.notes: East Belfast/Castlereagh. Popular for larger receptions.

currentVenueOptions.name: Malone Lodge Hotel
currentVenueOptions.notes: South Belfast. Popular with middle-class south Belfast families. Limited private room capacity.

currentVenueOptions.name: The Balmoral Hotel
currentVenueOptions.notes: South-west Belfast. Busy functions venue — less intimate feel.

currentVenueOptions.name: Church halls
currentVenueOptions.notes: Self-catering or volunteer catering. Low cost, limited facilities.

fernhillPositioning.proposedOffer: A dignified, garden-house wake reception venue — intimate, community-owned, with peaceful walled gardens for quiet reflection. Neither a hotel function suite nor a social club.
fernhillPositioning.cateringIncludes: Sandwiches, soup, traybakes, tea and coffee — traditional Belfast wake fare
uniqueFeatures: Walled garden for quiet reflection, fresh air, and gentle family gathering outdoors
Intimate domestic-scale rooms — not a cavernous hotel function suite
Community-owned — deeply resonant for south and west Belfast families
8–15 minutes from Milltown Cemetery and Belfast City Cemetery — natural post-service route
Optional remembrance garden: families can plant a tree or dedicate a memorial bench
Neutral, non-denominational — comfortable for all Belfast families
fernhillPositioning.weekdayAnchorNote: Wake receptions are needed year-round, primarily weekdays, with short notice — providing critical weekday revenue stability that weddings (weekend-only) cannot deliver.

citations.text: NISRA Deaths statistics hub, Northern Ireland 2022–2023
